name: Texas Dps
description_short: Texas DPS Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union serving the public since 1954, offering checking, savings, loans, credit cards, and investment services with no monthly fees.
description_long:
Texas DPS Credit Union was established in 1954 as a not-for-profit financial institution dedicated to serving those who serve others. Based in Austin, Texas, the credit union has built a decades-long reputation for member-focused service and competitive rates. The organization is federally insured by the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) and operates under NMLS #01624110, providing members with standard safety protections.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including deposit accounts (Simply Checking, youth accounts, savings accounts, holiday savings, and IRAs), various loan types (auto, recreational vehicle, home, home equity, and personal loans), credit and debit cards, share certificates, and investment/insurance planning services. They also provide convenience features like online and mobile banking, bill pay, direct deposit, and a 24-hour teller line. Their Easy Save Round Up Program automatically converts debit card transactions into savings.

Texas DPS Credit Union distinguishes itself through its member-centric approach, offering no monthly fees or minimum balances on checking accounts and competitive rates across product categories. Current advertised rates include auto loans as low as 4.74% APR, home equity loans at 5.24% APR, and share certificates up to 3.60% APY. The credit union emphasizes personalized service, with member testimonials highlighting quick loan approvals based on loyalty and consistent, professional customer service.

As a credit union, Texas DPS operates on a not-for-profit model where members are essentially owners, distinguishing it from commercial banks. However, like most credit unions, membership may be restricted based on eligibility criteria (the website mentions "Who Can Join" requirements). The organization actively engages members through annual membership days, financial literacy webinars, and community outreach, maintaining transparency through published policies and disclosures.
services: Youth Checking and Youth Membership Savings accounts
Holiday Savings and Enhanced Savings accounts
Home Loans and Home Equity Loans (from 5.24% APR)
Online and Mobile Banking with Bill Pay
pros: No monthly fees or minimum balances on Simply Checking account
Competitive loan rates: auto loans from 4.74% APR, home equity from 5.24% APR
Easy Save Round Up Program automatically converts debit card transactions into savings
Not-for-profit member-owned structure means profits benefit members, not shareholders
Member testimonials report quick loan approvals and personalized service based on loyalty
Federally insured by NCUA protecting deposits
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, loans, credit cards, IRAs, and investment services
cons: Membership eligibility may be restricted based on unstated criteria (specific member categories)
Limited physical branch locations (single address provided suggests limited branch network compared to larger banks)
Website does not clearly disclose membership eligibility requirements or fees for specific services
No information provided about credit limit ranges for credit cards or specific terms
Limited details on business account offerings despite serving diverse member base
best_for: Members eligible for Texas DPS Credit Union membership seeking no-fee checking and competitive loan rates
Consumers wanting to build savings habits through automated round-up programs with debit card usage
Borrowers with established loyalty or member status seeking fast personal loan approval
Individuals looking for a not-for-profit financial institution aligned with member ownership values
diagnosis: Texas DPS Credit Union is best for individuals who are eligible members seeking a not-for-profit alternative to traditional banks with competitive rates, no-fee checking, and personalized service. The main caveat is that membership eligibility is not clearly defined on the website, so prospective members must contact the credit union directly to confirm they qualify to join.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Texas Dps — Credit Union in Austin, TX | CreditDoc
meta_description: Texas Dps — NCUA-insured credit union in Austin, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
